rationale: The citing paper uses Fischer and Albers (2010) for more than a passing literature list mention. It is cited directly to support hypothesis framing about U.S. versus Europe differences in sales-force effectiveness (linked to DTC advertising availability), and it is also used as an explicit example in defining which primary-demand studies can still yield firm-level elasticity inputs for the meta-analysis. These are functional roles in conceptual setup and study-scope construction, even though the meta-analysis does not primarily build its method on that single paper.
relation_description: The meta-analysis draws on the cited study as prior empirical support for its geographic-setting hypothesis and as an example of a primary-demand study from which company-level personal-selling elasticities can be extracted for inclusion. Thus, the cited paper helps frame and operationalize parts of the meta-analytic design.
